bead.title: cleanup: report stale attempt descendant processes
bead.description:
PROBLEM
DDx cleanup dry-run can remove or report stale execution files, but it cannot tell an operator that a stale DDx-owned descendant process is still alive under a current-project execution worktree. This leaves orphan shells, cargo test or benchmark processes, and tiny long-lived test servers invisible during recovery.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` defines `ExecutionCleanupSummary` with file/resource counts only, so process findings, process-group counts, stale reasons, and `would_kill` status cannot be represented.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` cleans temp dirs, registered worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, agent logs, worker dirs, and candidate refs, but has no process census tied to DDx attempt worktrees.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-152` covers only PID-1 children whose command basename looks like a top-level harness and whose owner PID is gone; it does not report non-harness descendants left under stale execution worktrees.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` already gives normal harness children process-group and parent-death behavior, but cleanup has no later operator-visible census for descendants that survive after the harness root exits.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an internal, injectable stale attempt process scanner to execution cleanup. On Linux, collect `/proc` facts equivalent to the existing process-scanning style: pid, ppid, process group when available, command or truncated command, cwd, start time when available, and enough project ownership context to tie the process to the current project's DDx execution root or a known attempt worktree. Extend `ExecutionCleanupSummary` with process findings, counts, unavailable status, blocked warnings, stale reason, and `would_kill=true` for dry-run candidates. Dry-run must report candidates and must not call the killer. Add an unsupported-platform no-op/report-unavailable implementation. Detect non-harness descendants by cwd/worktree/liveness ownership evidence, not by broad command-name matching.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` only if this child changes the documented cleanup contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-bbea954f`; that bead owns dirty timeout worktree edit preservation before cleanup deletion.
- Do not replace the existing parent-death/process-group fixes from `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, or `ddx-418c3849`.
- Do not implement a general-purpose port scanner or host process manager. Port conflict recovery here comes only from safely reaping stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
- Do not kill processes outside the current project's DDx execution root, processes tied to live run-state/worker liveness, active registered worktrees, or non-DDx user workloads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- `spec:FEAT-010`
- `spec:SD-025`

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker reliability and Niflheim local gates).
Deps: none.
bead.acceptance:
1. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_DryRunReportsStaleAttemptDescendantProcesses` proving a stale process whose cwd/worktree is under the current project's DDx execution root is reported with pid, command, worktree, stale reason, and `would_kill=true`, while dry-run does not call the killer.
2. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_DetectsNonHarnessDescendantByOwnershipEvidence` proving a non-harness descendant command such as `sh`, `cargo`, or a tiny long-lived test server is detected by DDx ownership/cwd/liveness evidence, not broad command-name matching.
3. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_UnsupportedPlatformReportsProcessCleanupUnavailable` or an equivalent build-tagged assertion covering the deterministic unsupported-platform no-op/report-unavailable path.
4. Existing process-reaper behavior remains covered by `TestOrphanHarness*`, `TestExecutor*`, and `TestDockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup*` without weakening parent-death or process-group cleanup semantics.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'ExecutionCleanupManager_.*Process|AttemptDescendant|UnsupportedPlatform|OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

events.body:
no_changes
powerClass=standard
rationale: status: blocked
bead_id: ddx-47d9100a
attempt_id: 20260628T032845-7af8c094

AC1–AC5 satisfied
==================
All production code is correctly implemented and staged for commit:

  modified: cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go
  new file: cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go
  new file: cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_processes_linux.go
  new file: cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_processes_other.go

- ExecutionCleanupProcessFinding struct added (AC1)
- staleAttemptProcessScanner interface + injectable ProcessScanner/ProcessKiller (AC1)
- ExecutionCleanupSummary extended with StaleAttemptProcesses, StaleAttemptProcessCount,
  ProcessCleanupUnavailable (AC1)
- liveWorktrees map built from registered + runStates WorktreePaths (AC3)
- Linux /proc-based scanner (detection by cwd ownership, not command name) (AC2)
- Non-Linux no-op scanner returning unavailable=true (AC2)
- Dry-run: would_kill=true set but ProcessKiller NOT called (AC4)
- All three required tests pass: DryRunReports, DetectsNonHarness, UnsupportedPlatform (AC5)

AC5 verification:
  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'ExecutionCleanupManager_.*Process|AttemptDescendant|UnsupportedPlatform|OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1
  → PASS (0.24s)

AC6 blocked: pre-existing test failure outside bead scope
==========================================================
`lefthook run pre-commit` fails at the `go-test` step due to:

  FAIL: TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt
    execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:882
    Expected error with "context deadline exceeded" but got nil.

This failure is CONFIRMED PRE-EXISTING on the base revision (edbcd9aa5) before any
of this bead's changes. Verified by:
  git stash && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt -count=1
  → FAIL (same failure)

Root cause: The test was added in commit 4555a083f (test(agent): add Niflheim retry
regressions [ddx-df319cf9]) using a 150ms fixed timeout. The adjacent test
(TestPreDispatchDirtyPreservePersistsStableImplementationDirt, ~line 726) was already
updated to use the correct require.Eventually + goroutine + cancel pattern (see the
comment at line 772-774 explaining why 750ms was too tight). The failing test was not
similarly updated.

Fix required (out-of-scope for ddx-47d9100a): Update
execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt
to use require.Eventually polling + goroutine pattern with a generous outer timeout
(2-30s), matching the pattern used by the test immediately above it.

This bead's changes do not introduce any new test failures (verified by running both
failing tests on base revision vs. with changes: same failure count and same failure).
